events.description: A Hezbollah-launched Shahed-type kamikaze drone from Lebanon strikes RAF Akrotiri air base in Cyprus, hitting a runway hangar. Minor damage reported, no casualties. First attack on a European NATO base during the conflict.
A second drone launched from Lebanon toward Cyprus is intercepted by Greek F-16 fighter jets over Cypriot airspace, not Lebanese airspace as originally stated.

UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer dispatches HMS Dragon, a Type 45 air defense destroyer equipped with Sea Viper missile systems, along with helicopters with counter-drone capabilities, to reinforce Cyprus.

Greece deploys two frigates and four F-16 fighter jets to Cyprus in response to drone strikes on RAF Akrotiri. France agrees to send frigate Languedoc and land-based anti-drone and anti-missile systems.
Unidentified drone sighted near Larnaca International Airport forces temporary closure. Over 60 flights cancelled at Larnaca and Paphos airports as Iran conflict disrupts Cyprus air traffic.

Italy, Spain, Netherlands, and Germany announce they will send naval assets to protect Cyprus. France deploys its flagship aircraft carrier to the eastern Mediterranean in response to the widening conflict.

Thousands of Cypriots protest the existence of British military bases on the island, demanding removal of UK bases and an end to military ties with the US-Israel coalition. Demonstrators fear Cyprus being dragged into the Iran conflict.
29 more flights cancelled at Larnaca airport on Saturday alone. Tourism sector, which accounts for 14% of Cyprus GDP, faces severe disruption as airlines suspend routes to the island.

Two Iranian one-way attack drones strike Azerbaijan's Nakhchivan exclave. One hits the Razi airport terminal building, another falls near a school in Shakarabad village. At least two civilians injured.
events.civilian_impact: At least two civilians injured in drone strikes on Razi airport terminal and near school in Shakarabad village

events.description: President Aliyev calls Iranian drone strike on Nakhchivan a 'terrorist act' and orders Azerbaijani army to full combat readiness. Azerbaijan threatens retaliatory measures against Iran.

Azerbaijan recalls all diplomatic staff from Iran following the Nakhchivan drone strike. Iran denies responsibility, with its foreign minister suggesting an Israeli false flag operation to draw Azerbaijan into the war.

NATO integrated air defense systems intercept an Iranian ballistic missile entering Turkish airspace. Debris from the interceptor falls in the Dortyol district of Hatay Province. No casualties. Iran claims a 'technical anomaly.'

NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte states alliance is committed to defending Turkey after missile debris falls in Hatay Province. Turkey asserts right to self-defense. Article 4 consultations discussed between Turkish FM Fidan and allied leadership.

Iranian missiles and drones target Muwaffaq Salti Air Base in Jordan, over 500 miles from Iran. The AN/TPY-2 X-band radar for a US THAAD missile defense battery is destroyed, a system valued at nearly $500 million.

Satellite imagery confirms the THAAD radar at Muwaffaq Salti was struck on March 1-2. Without the AN/TPY-2 radar, wide-area ballistic missile defense coverage across Israel, Jordan, and the Levant corridor is significantly degraded.

Pro-Iran protesters storm the US consulate in Karachi, breaching the outer wall. US Marine Security Guards open fire on the crowd. At least 10 killed and over 60 injured at the consulate. Protesters also set fire to a traffic post and threw stones.
events.civilian_impact: 10 killed, over 60 injured at US consulate in Karachi

events.description: At least 13 protesters and security personnel killed in pro-Iran demonstrations in Gilgit and Skardu in northern Pakistan. Shiite demonstrators attack offices of the UN Military Observer Group in Azad Kashmir.
events.civilian_impact: At least 13 killed (protesters and security personnel) in pro-Iran demonstrations in Gilgit and Skardu

events.description: Two protesters killed near the US Embassy in Islamabad during pro-Iran marches. Total death toll across Pakistan reaches at least 24 with dozens more injured.
events.civilian_impact: Two protesters killed near the US Embassy in Islamabad during pro-Iran marches

events.description: Pakistan deploys military troops and imposes a three-day curfew in Gilgit, Skardu, and Shigar districts following deadly protests. Army deployed to suppress escalation of violence.

India activates 'Project Ghar' (Project Home) evacuation operation for 9 million Indian nationals in Gulf Cooperation Council countries. Ministry of External Affairs puts Indian Navy and Air Force on standby for mass evacuations.

Over 52,000 Indians evacuated from Gulf states by March 7, with 32,107 travelling on Indian carriers. Hundreds of thousands more await evacuation flights as airports in the region face disruptions and cancellations.

Egyptian President el-Sisi declares Egypt in a 'state of near-emergency' as the Iran war threatens the economy. Warns of runaway inflation and Suez Canal disruption. Three major shipping companies halt passage through the canal.

CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd, and Maersk reroute ships around the Cape of Good Hope, bypassing both the Strait of Hormuz and the Suez Canal. Egypt's Suez Canal revenues, already down 60% from 2024, face further devastation.
Brent crude oil surges 12% to over $92 per barrel as Iran closes the Strait of Hormuz. US crude tops $91. Roughly one-third of seaborne crude oil trade and 19% of global LNG flows transit the strait.

Maritime insurers cancel war risk protection and indemnity insurance for vessels transiting the Persian Gulf effective March 5. Economic risk becomes too high for ship owners. Over 400 vessels anchor outside the strait.

Trump announces US Development Finance Corporation will provide political risk insurance for maritime trade through the Gulf. States US Navy will begin escorting tankers through the Strait of Hormuz 'as soon as possible.'

European benchmark natural gas futures spike, with Goldman Sachs warning prices could more than double if Hormuz shipments are halted for over two months. European energy security threatened as LNG supplies from Qatar are disrupted.

Over 400,000 metric tons of Indian basmati rice for export stuck at Indian ports or in transit. 75% of India's basmati exports (6 million tons/year) go to the Middle East. Shipping rerouted via Cape of Good Hope, adding 14-25 days to transit.

Fire breaks out at Jebel Ali Port in Dubai attributed to debris from aerial interception of an Iranian drone attack. Jebel Ali is the largest port in the Middle East and a critical global shipping hub.
A Shahed-type drone strikes near the Fairmont The Palm Hotel on Palm Jumeirah in Dubai, causing a large explosion and fire. Four people injured from debris and blast effects.
events.civilian_impact: Four people injured from debris and blast effects in strike near Fairmont The Palm Hotel

events.description: Iranian drones strike AWS data centers in the UAE, setting facility mec1-az2 on fire. Power shut down. Amazon confirms two UAE data centers and a Bahrain facility damaged by drone strikes, taking them offline and disrupting digital services across the region.

Iranian drones strike the French naval air base Camp de la Paix near Zayed Port in Abu Dhabi. The strike on a French military facility in the Gulf broadens the international dimensions of the conflict.

UAE Defense Ministry reports intercepting 161 of 174 tracked ballistic missiles, 645 of 689 detected drones, and 8 cruise missiles since February 28. An additional 812 drones detected with 755 intercepted; 57 made impact within the country. Four foreign nationals killed and 112 injured overall.
events.civilian_impact: Four foreign nationals killed and 112 injured overall from Iranian strikes since February 28

events.description: UAE considers striking Iranian missile launch sites as over 800 projectiles target the country. Four foreign nationals killed (from Pakistan, Nepal, and Bangladesh) and 112 injured since strikes began.
events.civilian_impact: Four foreign nationals killed and 112 injured since strikes began

events.description: Hezbollah launches 23 separate attacks against Israel in 24 hours using rockets (16 incidents) and UAVs (7 incidents), targeting the border communities, the Galilee, the Golan Heights, and the Haifa area.
Rocket sirens sound in Kiryat Shmona as Hezbollah launches rockets and suspected drone infiltrations across the Galilee Panhandle and Golan Heights. Multiple impacts reported in border communities.

Hezbollah projectile fired near the Lebanese border injures 8 Israeli soldiers, five severely. A follow-up rocket attack damages a Puma armored engineering vehicle and hits a Caterpillar D9 bulldozer attempting rescue, killing two IDF soldiers.

US and Israeli strikes target Iranian air bases at Shiraz, Dezful, and Tabriz, destroying hangars and runway infrastructure. Israeli Air Force deploys approximately 200 fighter jets in the largest IAF combat sortie in history.

IAEA confirms entrance buildings at Iran's underground Natanz Fuel Enrichment Plant were damaged in US-Israeli strikes. Attacks targeted enrichment facility entrances at the main Iranian nuclear site. No fatalities reported.

US-Israeli strikes damage the Isfahan Nuclear Technology Center irradiation facility. No radiation leak reported. Strikes also hit a covert weapons development site known as Minzadehei and the Lavisan 2/Mojdeh complex.

Israel strikes over 600 regime targets using 2,500 munitions in the first 48 hours, hitting over 20 security leadership targets, more than 150 ballistic missile sites, and over 200 Iranian air-defense systems.

US claims destruction of IRGC command center. Operation expands to Iran's internal security architecture, striking IRGC, Basij, police, intelligence, and special forces sites used to suppress protests in multiple cities.

Israel's army chief of staff announces Israel has destroyed 80% of Iran's air defenses and at least 60% of its ability to launch missiles. Total of approximately 5,000 bombs dropped since the start of the war.

Washington Post reports Russia providing Iran with satellite imagery showing locations and movements of US troops, ships, and aircraft. Officials describe a 'comprehensive effort' to share targeting intelligence on American military assets.

US officials confirm Russia has shared detailed intelligence with Iran including positions of American warships, aircraft deployments, and military infrastructure across the Middle East, sourced from Moscow's satellite constellation.

CENTCOM strikes the IRIS Shahid Bagheri, Iran's recently commissioned drone and helicopter carrier, in the Persian Gulf within hours of launching Operation Epic Fury. Vessel hit twice amidships and on the port quarter.

US strikes on Bandar Abbas and Konarak naval bases destroy the core of the Iranian Navy's surface fleet in under 48 hours, including both Moudge-class frigates, the IRIS Makran forward base ship, and multiple smaller vessels.

A Jamaran-class corvette confirmed sunk at pier in Chabahar on the Gulf during the initial phase of Operation Epic Fury. Smoke rises from the naval wharf at Konarak Naval Base. Iran's entire Gulf of Oman fleet of 11 warships eliminated within 48 hours.

CENTCOM announces destruction of over 30 Iranian ships total. The IRGCN's Soleimani-class corvette, the first of its class, is confirmed sunk. Iran's naval capacity to threaten Gulf shipping effectively eliminated.

Iran launches hundreds of drones targeting Kuwait, Iraq, Saudi Arabia, and UAE simultaneously. Hundreds of explosions reported across the Gulf as Iranian forces attempt to overwhelm air defenses of multiple nations.

UK agrees to let the United States access RAF Akrotiri and Dhekelia bases in Cyprus for strikes on Iranian missiles and launch sites, but excludes use for strikes on political and economic targets in Iran.

Israeli natural gas exports from the Tamar and Leviathan offshore fields suspended due to security concerns. Egypt forced to suspend gas flows to Lebanon and Syria through the Arab Gas Pipeline to prioritize domestic supply.

Asia-Pacific nations including China, Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, Indonesia, and Australia activate emergency operations to evacuate citizens from the Persian Gulf and secure energy supplies. Multiple countries monitor financial markets.

US State Department urges all Americans to leave 14 countries in the Middle East as the Iran war escalates. Commercial flights cancelled across the region. Airlines suspend routes to multiple Gulf destinations.

IRGC commander Abdolhamid Rahbar of the 15 Khordad Air Defense Group and four other members killed in an Israeli strike in Isfahan. The 15 Khordad group operates Iran's domestically produced air defense systems.

Iran's ballistic missile launch rate has dropped 90% and drone launch rate 83% since day one, suggesting severe depletion of stocks. Iran has fired over 500 ballistic and naval missiles and almost 2,000 drones since February 28.

Iran targets THAAD missile defense radars across multiple countries. Strikes hit radar installations, satellite communications terminals, and radomes in Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Jordan, and Saudi Arabia in coordinated campaign against US sensor network.

Amnesty International calls for urgent investigation into killings during Pakistan protests. Total confirmed dead across the country reaches at least 24, with human rights groups questioning use of lethal force by Marines and security services.

Oxford Economics estimates eurozone growth reduced by 0.1% with inflation up 0.5% due to the conflict. Most Asian economies face half a percentage point inflation rise if Brent crude remains elevated above $90.

Additional drones detected approaching Cyprus from the east. Greek F-16s and newly deployed European air defense systems intercept multiple threats. Drones confirmed to be of Iranian origin launched from Lebanon.

Nvidia and Amazon temporarily close Dubai offices. Google employees stranded. Major tech companies begin emergency relocations as Iranian strikes on UAE data centers and infrastructure threaten the Gulf's status as a tech hub.

US Secretary of Defense Hegseth dismisses speculation that the Turkish missile incident might trigger NATO Article 5, stating the US is 'just getting started' in Iran. Article 4 consultations remain under discussion.

Multiple Iranian missiles intercepted over Saudi Arabia. Saudi air defenses engage incoming ballistic missiles and drones targeting both military installations and urban areas. Sirens sound across Riyadh and the Eastern Province.

Egypt activates national crisis operations room for hourly coordination among ministries tracking energy, food, security, and consular developments. Suspension of Israeli gas exports forces Egypt to ration domestic natural gas supply.

metadata.conflict_name: 2026 Iran War (Operation Epic Fury) - Expanded Events
metadata.description: Supplementary events covering fronts and dimensions not included in the primary events.json dataset.
fronts_covered: Cyprus (RAF Akrotiri drone strikes, European military deployments, flight cancellations, protests)
Turkey (NATO missile interception, Hatay Province debris)
Jordan (THAAD radar destruction at Muwaffaq Salti Air Base)
Pakistan (Karachi consulate attack, nationwide protests, 24+ killed)
Strait of Hormuz (Insurance withdrawal, 400+ ships stuck, tanker traffic collapse)
Global economic impact (Oil price spike, European gas prices, Indian basmati rice exports)
European military deployments (UK, France, Greece, Italy, Spain, Netherlands, Germany)
UAE expanded (Jebel Ali fire, Palm Jumeirah strike, AWS data center attack, 800+ projectiles)
Hezbollah expanded (23 attacks in 24 hours, Kiryat Shmona, IDF casualties)
Israel strikes on Iran expanded (Natanz, Isfahan nuclear, air bases, IRGC internal security)
Russia intelligence sharing (Satellite imagery, US force locations)
Naval operations expanded (Shahid Bagheri drone carrier, 30+ ships destroyed, fleet elimination)
metadata.notes: These events supplement the primary events.json (48 events) with no duplicates. Casualty figures are reported numbers and may be revised. Coordinates are approximate site-level locations.
